title: Implemented Morrohsu Unix-Style CLI Agent Architecture
rationale: Addressed three critical failure modes: binary file flooding, silent stderr, and large output overwhelming attention. Established two-layer architecture: raw Unix Execution Layer and LLM Presentation Layer.

content: Binary detection prevents garbage bytes in context using IsBinary() function with null byte, UTF-8 validity, and control character ratio checks. Provides human-readable error messages with recovery guidance.
stderr is always visible on command failures or when stderr buffer contains content. This ensures agents immediately see error messages like 'command not found' and can respond appropriately.
Large outputs are truncated at 200 lines or 50KB with automatic spillover to temporary files (/tmp/fernwood-output/) and exploration hints for accessing full content.
All command results include metadata footer [exit:N | Xms] providing clear success/failure signals and performance awareness for agents.
Unified 'run' tool replaces multiple discrete tools with single command registry. Maintains backward compatibility through shell fallback for unregistered commands.

name: Morrohsu Specification
description: Unix-Style CLI Agent Architecture specification addressing binary file flooding, silent stderr, and large output issues with two-layer architecture.

title: Evolutionary Approach to Unified Command Registry
rationale: Rather than replacing existing individual tools, enhance shell execution with Morrohsu protections and gradually introduce unified command capabilities. This preserves backward compatibility while enabling architectural evolution.

title: Evolutionary Enhancement Over Revolutionary Replacement
rationale: Working within existing Fernwood architecture preserves functionality while incrementally adding Morrohsu protections. This approach reduces risk and maintains compatibility while enabling gradual improvement.
